sourceMovie.title: Hard Rain
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Hard Rain for the same reason it broke through in 1998: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a crime rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Mikael Salomon keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The thriller and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Transporter
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Hard Rain clicked for you, The Transporter (2002) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Hard Rain?
faq.answer: Try The Transporter, Rebel Ridge, Takers—each shares DNA with Hard Rain while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Hard Rain?
faq.answer: Hard Rain (1998) is best described as Thriller, Crime, Action. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Hard Rain worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this thriller lane.
